title: [FABRIC][P1.1] Build Sublist trie-based routing engine
description:
## Overview
Build a trie-based subject matching engine in src/messaging/subject.rs (routing portion), inspired by NATS server/sublist.go.

## Requirements

### Subject Matching
- Subjects are dot-separated token paths (e.g., `foo.bar.baz`)
- Support literal match: exact token equality at each level
- Support single-token wildcard (`*`): matches exactly one token at that position
- Support tail wildcard (`>`): matches one or more tokens from that position to end of subject
- Examples: `foo.*` matches `foo.bar` but not `foo.bar.baz`; `foo.>` matches `foo.bar` and `foo.bar.baz`

### Trie Structure
- Internal trie nodes keyed by token string
- Each node has: children map, wildcard child (`*`), tail-wildcard child (`>`), and subscriber set
- Insert/remove operations maintain structural invariants (prune empty branches on remove)
- Lookup walks the trie, collecting all matching subscribers across literal, wildcard, and tail-wildcard branches

### Caching
- Generation-invalidated literal-subject hot caches: cache full match results for literal (no-wildcard) subjects
- Per-link hot caches: each link (connection/session) maintains its own cache of recently resolved subjects
- Cache entries tagged with a generation counter; any mutation to the trie bumps the generation, invalidating all caches
- Cache hit path should be sub-microsecond (simple HashMap lookup + generation check)

### Concurrency
- Thread-safe concurrent reads with write-serialized mutations
- Read path: lock-free for cached lookups (AtomicU64 generation check + cached result)
- Write path: single writer at a time (Mutex or similar), bumps generation after mutation
- No reader starvation: writes must not block reads for extended periods

### Cancel-Correctness
- Interest registration is cancel-correct: when a subscriber is cancelled (Cx cancelled, task dropped), its interest MUST be removed from the trie
- No ghost interest: a cancelled subscriber must never appear in match results
- Implementation: tie subscriber lifetime to a guard/handle that removes interest on drop
- Test: subscribe, cancel the subscriber's Cx, verify the subscriber no longer appears in any match results

### Queue Groups
- Multiple subscribers can register under the same subject with a queue group name
- For a given message, only ONE subscriber per queue group receives delivery
- Queue group selection should be round-robin or random (not always the same subscriber)
- A subscriber can be in at most one queue group per subject

### Performance
- O(tokens) lookup where tokens = number of dot-separated segments in the subject
- Cache hit should be sub-microsecond
- Benchmark: 1M lookups/sec for cached literal subjects on modern hardware

## File Location
src/messaging/subject.rs (routing portion)

## Tests (in this file or tests/)
- Literal match: exact match, no match, partial match
- Single wildcard: `*` at various positions, multiple wildcards
- Tail wildcard: `>` at various depths, `>` alone matches everything
- Combined: `foo.*.>`, `*.bar.>`, etc.
- Edge cases: empty subject, single token, very deep nesting (100+ levels)
- Queue groups: single group delivery, multiple groups, group removal
- Cache: hit/miss, invalidation on mutation, generation rollover
- Concurrency: concurrent subscribe/unsubscribe/lookup from multiple threads
- Cancel-correctness: subscribe, drop guard, verify removal
- At least 20+ test cases total

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text: NATS implementation reference: server/sublist.go implements the trie-based matching. Key patterns to adapt: (1) the Sublist struct with Insert/Match/Remove operations, (2) result caching with generation-based invalidation, (3) queue group handling where one subscriber per group receives each message, (4) the SublistResult type with subscription lists. The Asupersync version must additionally make interest registration cancel-correct — NATS doesn't have this because Go's GC handles cleanup. In Asupersync, a cancelled subscriber MUST NOT leave ghost interest behind, which means interest removal must participate in the cancel/drain/finalize protocol.
